categories.summary:
# Analysis of 💻│developers Channel

## 1. Summary
The discussion primarily focused on UI/UX improvements for the Hyperfy platform. Ashxn introduced a new "keepActive" option that allows apps to remain live while being moved, maintaining their script execution, scale, and rotation. This solves issues with apps resetting to their unscripted state during movement. The conversation then shifted to a major UI overhaul, with Ashxn sharing Figma mockups for a redesigned interface that separates functionality for regular players versus builders. The proposed design includes a cleaner hierarchy with dedicated sections for world settings, documentation, app management, and app configuration. 0mka and Ashxn debated implementation approaches, with 0mka suggesting additional refactoring of the codebase structure while Ashxn preferred focusing solely on the UI changes. The discussion also touched on improving the world folder structure to make it more git-friendly and AI-friendly, with proposals for organizing blueprints, scripts, and assets in a more logical manner. JollyGrin inquired about editing code outside the in-world editor, revealing current limitations in the workflow that the team is working to address.

## 2. FAQ
Q: Does the keepActive option solve keeping the scale size of something when you are moving it? (asked by 0mka) A: Yes (answered by Ashxn)
Q: Do we still get the keybinds UI? Space to double jump etc? (asked by peezy) A: Unanswered
Q: What do you think about pros and cons of folder per app versus scripts/assets/(blueprints or manifests)? (asked by peezy) A: Ashxn indicated they were thinking the same thing, with blueprints essentially being manifests pointing to models, scripts, and prop assets with metadata and flags.
Q: How can I add code via the editor (not in world) and have it show up in the app menu? (asked by JollyGrin) A: Ashxn explained the world folder is an engine/artifact format not meant to be manually changed; users should drag and drop things into the world and let the engine handle storage.
Q: Is there a way to do this via code? (asked by JollyGrin) A: Ashxn mentioned this is something they've been discussing as they all want version control of some kind.

## 3. Help Interactions
Helper: Ashxn | Helpee: JollyGrin | Context: JollyGrin was trying to understand how to add code via external editor rather than in-world editor | Resolution: Ashxn explained that the world folder format isn't meant to be manually edited, and that version control features are being discussed but not yet implemented.
Helper: Ashxn | Helpee: 0mka | Context: 0mka asked if the keepActive option solves scale issues when moving objects | Resolution: Ashxn confirmed it does solve this problem.
Helper: Ashxn | Helpee: Community | Context: Sharing UI redesign mockups to address confusing hierarchy in current interface | Resolution: Provided Figma mockups showing cleaner separation between player and builder interfaces.

## 4. Action Items
Type: Technical | Description: Implement the "keepActive" option for apps to remain live while being moved | Mentioned By: Ashxn
Type: Technical | Description: Redesign UI to separate regular player view from builder view with improved hierarchy | Mentioned By: Ashxn
Type: Technical | Description: Implement folder structure improvements for better organization of world assets | Mentioned By: Ashxn, peezy
Type: Feature | Description: Add version control capabilities for app development | Mentioned By: Ashxn, JollyGrin
Type: Feature | Description: Create a more git-friendly and AI-friendly world folder structure | Mentioned By: Ashxn, peezy
Type: Documentation | Description: Document the new UI structure and navigation for builders | Mentioned By: 0mka
Type: Technical | Description: Implement documentation tab in the new UI | Mentioned By: 0mka, Ashxn
Type: Technical | Description: Add palette selection in the new UI | Mentioned By: Ashxn

# Discord Chat Analysis for "⚡│general"

## 1. Summary:
The chat segment contains minimal technical discussion. Users primarily shared their "hype dreams" - desired features they'd like to see in Hyperfy. Maximus described an object importer that would allow users to bring items from their blockchain wallets or external data sources into worlds without gaining builder permissions. Agent12 proposed "Engage-MINT Systems" that would add value to interactable objects, enabling rogue-like growth games where interactions mint tokens on a curve. These tokens could be spent on in-world NFTs or upgrades. Agent12 also suggested enhancing the "Summon App" with "hittable objects" or different "skins" for throwable items. Jin mentioned summarizing three months of Hyperfy Discord activity into daily reports. The conversation included some joking references to the platform being "dead," which MUDBONE clarified was an ongoing joke.

## 2. FAQ:
Q: If there was one hype you wanted for a world you are building in, what would it be? (asked by 0mka) A: Maximus described an object importer for blockchain wallets/data sources; Agent12 described "Engage-MINT Systems" for adding value to interactable objects
Q: What do you mean 'hittable objects'? (asked by 0mka) A: Objects that can be thrown to hit and destroy other objects, with the app providing them when set down (answered by Agent12)

## 3. Help Interactions:
Helper: 0mka | Helpee: Agent12 | Context: Clarification needed on "hittable objects" concept | Resolution: Agent12 explained the concept of throwable objects that can destroy other objects in the game environment

## 4. Action Items:
Feature: Object importer for blockchain wallets/external data sources without granting builder permissions | Description: Allow users to bring objects into worlds while letting world owners control supported object types | Mentioned By: maximus
Feature: Engage-MINT Systems | Description: Add value to interactable objects where interactions mint tokens on a curve for in-world purchases | Mentioned By: Agent12
Feature: Enhanced Summon App | Description: Add "hittable objects" and different "skins" for throwable items | Mentioned By: Agent12
Documentation: Discord summary reports | Description: Daily reports of Hyperfy Discord activity from 2025-01-01 to present | Mentioned By: jin

# Analysis of Discord Chat in "🪙│hyper" Channel

## 1. Summary
This chat segment contains no technical discussions, decisions, or problem-solving. The conversation is entirely focused on price movement of what appears to be a cryptocurrency or token. Users are commenting on price drops, mentioning "paper hands" (a term for investors who sell quickly during price declines), and sharing their recent purchases at specific price points. One user jokes that their purchase will likely cause the price to drop further, while another encourages continued buying.

## 2. FAQ
No technical questions or meaningful responses were present in this chat segment.

## 3. Help Interactions
No significant help interactions were observed in this chat segment.

## 4. Action Items
No technical tasks, documentation needs, or feature requests were mentioned in this conversation.

The chat segment is extremely brief, containing only a single message from user "peezy" announcing that the creators of tripoSG have released new tools, including one for rigging. The message includes two emoji reactions and a link to another Discord channel message. No technical discussions, problem-solving, or implementations are present in this limited segment.
